//: RELEASED 2XKO patches only — the per-patch authority scripts/patches.ts derives
windows from. Seasons stay in seasonBoundaries.json (the season authority);
patches NEST UNDER SEASONS BY RELEASE DATE, never by version digits.
VERSION SCHEME (confirmed 2026-07-23): <season>.<act>.<patch>. The FIRST digit
is the season — every 1.x.y patch is Season 1 material (1.0.x = the Early
Access run Riot branded 'Season 0'). The MIDDLE digit is the ACT within the
season, NOT a season: 1.2.x is Season 1 act 2. The community repeatedly reads
act bumps as season changes (channels branded 1.2.1 'Season 2' and pre-hyped
'Season 3') — never take a channel's word for a season transition; only
seasonBoundaries.json (from Riot's explicit announcements) opens a season.
Rules:
- Author from Riot's game-updates page. Riot skips numbers (no 1.0.2 / 1.1.4 /
1.2.2 / 1.2.4 exist) — NEVER invent a version to fill a sequence gap.
- Hotfixes fold into their parent patch; record them in `includes` (they never
get their own row — replays expire on every patch change incl. hotfixes, but
the community talks in parent-patch terms).
- `start` is the release day (ISO). A patch's window ends at the next patch's
start within the same season, at the season boundary for a season's last
patch, open-ended for the newest (windows are computed, never authored).
- Each season's opening patch must start ON the season's start date
(validated; rows carrying `todo` are exempt while unconfirmed).
- `note` is a short community-facing hint (new champion, headline change),
surfaced in the filter dropdown.
